articles.title: 20 Times Square Loan Returns to Special Servicing
articles.narrative: The $647.5 million CMBS loan secured by a 99-year ground lease on the 20 Times Square property has returned to special servicing after missing its May 2026 maturity date. The loan, originated by Natixis in 2018, previously entered special servicing in November 2022 before receiving an extension in late 2023. The property includes a 454-room hotel and largely vacant retail space, with the retail portion having previously housed a significant tenant that closed shortly after its 2018 debut.

data_points.project_notes: The loan is secured by a 99-year ground lease that runs through 2117. The retail space was previously anchored by a 43,130-square-foot NFL Experience store, which closed shortly after its 2018 debut. The hotel component was shuttered during the COVID-19 pandemic and reopened in June 2021.

articles.title: Canero Flips Condo in Miami’s Coconut Grove to El-Ad for $46M
articles.narrative: Jose Canero has sold the Chateau Grove Condominium in Miami's Coconut Grove for $45.5 million to El-Ad National Properties. Canero had previously acquired the 25-unit building for $17.3 million and terminated its condo association, a move that grants the buyer full control for redevelopment. El-Ad National Properties intends to redevelop the property, recognizing Coconut Grove as a highly desirable and established neighborhood in Miami.

data_points.project_notes: The 2.6-acre site was previously acquired for $17.3 million by the seller, who also terminated the condo association.

articles.title: Hyatt in SoCal’s Inland Empire Secures $104M to Finance Upgrades
articles.narrative: National Core, a nonprofit development firm, has secured $103.6 million in tax-exempt bond financing for the renovation of the Hyatt Regency hotel in Ontario, CA. The financing package, which includes tax-exempt hotel revenue bonds and C-PACE bonds, will fund a significant redevelopment of the previously distressed property. The project will reduce the room count from 309 to 295 by enlarging suites, and will also include a lobby redesign, new food and beverage options, and upgraded amenities.

data_points.notable_features: Located less than one mile from Ontario International Airport and Toyota Arena, and near the Brightline West high-speed rail terminal.
data_points.project_notes: Acquired through a bankruptcy auction in August 2024. The financing represents approximately 80 percent of the total project cost with a 35-year fixed interest rate, including a five-year, interest-only period.

articles.title: Father-Son Retail Team Michael and Brian Hirschfeld Join Colliers
articles.narrative: Veteran retail broker Michael Hirschfeld and his son Brian Hirschfeld have joined Colliers' New York City operations, departing from JLL. Michael will serve as vice chair, focusing on luxury retail leasing, while Brian, as senior vice president, will leverage his expertise in national expansion strategies. This move is expected to significantly enhance Colliers' retail services in New York and beyond, given their extensive experience, including leasing approximately 1 million square feet along the Las Vegas Strip.

people.name: John Gallagher
people.title: Executive Managing Director of New York City and Brokerage and Market Leader

articles.title: SakeBrooklyn to Open 7K-SF Brewery at Property Markets Group’s Society Brooklyn
articles.narrative: SakeBrooklyn has leased 6,500 square feet for a new brewery and taproom at Society Brooklyn, a mixed-use multifamily development in Gowanus, Brooklyn. The new location at 500 Degraw Street will feature a full-scale brewing operation and a public-facing taproom, adding to the growing retail landscape along the Gowanus Canal. The tenant's broker noted that the increasing residential density in Gowanus is driving demand for thoughtful retail activations that create destinations.

data_points.address: 500 Degraw Street, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Located on the northern corner of a 605,000-square-foot multifamily development with 517 apartments, including market-rate and affordable housing units, and ground-floor retail spaces along Bond, Union, and Degraw streets, with a public plaza along the Gowanus Canal.

articles.market_intelligence: The average retail asking rents in the Gowanus area were $70 per square foot as of November.

articles.title: Outrigger Industrial Sells 1 MSF Building at Generation Park in Northeast Houston
articles.narrative: Outrigger Industrial has sold a 1 million-square-foot industrial building located within the Generation Park master-planned development in northeast Houston. The buyer and sales price were not disclosed. Additionally, Outrigger Industrial has secured a full-building lease with Representative Materials Co. for its other 255,871-square-foot industrial facility within the same development.

data_points.notable_features: Features 40-foot clear heights, ESFR sprinkler systems, and abundant car and trailer parking.

articles.title: TruCore Industrial Acquires 72,710 SF Industrial Portfolio in Houston, San Antonio
articles.narrative: TruCore Industrial has acquired a two-property industrial portfolio totaling 72,710 square feet, with assets located in Houston and San Antonio. Both properties were fully leased to a power distribution and control equipment manufacturer at the time of sale. The transaction details, including the seller and sale price, were not disclosed.

articles.title: LanCarte Commercial Arranges Sale of 33,000 SF Industrial Building in South Fort Worth
articles.narrative: LanCarte Commercial has facilitated the sale of a 33,000-square-foot industrial building located in South Fort Worth's Carter Industrial Park. The single-tenant property at 7610 Will Rogers Blvd. was acquired by an undisclosed buyer who intends to utilize the facility for manufacturing operations. Sarah LanCarte represented the undisclosed seller in this transaction.

data_points.address: 7610 Will Rogers Blvd, Fort Worth, TX
data_points.notable_features: Single-tenant building located within Carter Industrial Park.

articles.title: Remedi SeniorCare Signs 10,248 SF Lease Renewal at Flex Building in Plano
articles.narrative: Remedi SeniorCare of Dallas has renewed its 10,248-square-foot lease at 2600 Research Drive, a flex building in Plano. This renewal ensures the continued occupancy of the space by the senior care provider.

articles.title: Guardian Acquires 332-Unit Ladd Tower Apartment Building in Downtown Portland
articles.narrative: Guardian has acquired Ladd Tower, a 332-unit Class A apartment building in downtown Portland, from an undisclosed seller. The property's prime location adjacent to the South Park Blocks and its proximity to Portland State University, public transit, and major employers were key attractions for the buyer.

data_points.notable_features: 23-story Class A apartment building adjacent to the South Park Blocks and within walking distance of Portland State University, public transit, and major downtown employers.

articles.title: Joseph Nicholas Construction breaks ground on Cadillac dealership in Chicago’s former Lincoln Yards site
articles.narrative: Joseph Nicholas Construction (JNC), in partnership with Jack Carter Auto Group, has broken ground on Carter Cadillac of Chicago, a new flagship dealership located at 1400 W. North Ave. in Chicago. The development, part of the former Lincoln Yards site, will feature a two-story showroom and a six-level parking garage with 490 spaces. This facility is slated to be the brand's third flagship store globally and is expected to open in late 2027, aiming to accelerate business development and job creation in the city.

data_points.notable_features: Third Cadillac flagship store globally; authorized to sell the 2026 Cadillac CELESTIQ ultra-luxury sedan.
data_points.project_notes: Located on a vacant lot at the corner of North Avenue and Besly Court, part of the former Lincoln Yards site.

articles.market_intelligence: Construction in the U.S. auto dealership market is being driven by increased renovation and new builds for luxury, import, and domestic stores.

articles.title: Bradford Allen celebrates grand opening of 301-unit apartment community in Arlington Heights
articles.narrative: Bradford Allen has celebrated the grand opening of Arbor House, a new 301-unit luxury apartment community in Arlington Heights, IL. The eight-story development offers studio, one-, two-, and three-bedroom apartments with upscale finishes and extensive indoor and outdoor amenities, including a fitness center, resident lounge with golf simulator, coworking suites, and a private outdoor terrace with a pool. The community also features 26,000 square feet of ground-floor retail and serves as the anchor of Bradford Allen's larger 18-acre mixed-use Arlington Gateway district, which includes a renovated 150,000-square-foot wellness center.

data_points.address: 25 E. Algonquin Road, Arlington Heights, IL
data_points.notable_features: Luxury apartment community with 26,000 square feet of ground-floor retail, extensive amenities including a pool, spa, golf simulator, and coworking suites. Concrete construction provides sound insulation. Located at the southeast gateway to Arlington Heights with views of Busse Woods and the Chicago skyline.
data_points.project_notes: Anchor of Bradford Allen's 18-acre mixed-use Arlington Gateway district, which includes the renovated 150,000-square-foot Arlington Med wellness center. Located near Busse Woods nature preserve, Jane Addams Memorial Tollway, and Arlington Heights Metra station.

articles.title: The NRP Group marks groundbreaking of 348-unit affordable housing community in Southeast Austin
articles.narrative: The NRP Group and the Housing Authority of Travis County have broken ground on Sanara, a 348-unit affordable housing community in Southeast Austin, TX. This development, located on a 56-acre site with 18 acres dedicated to residential use, will provide housing for households earning up to 60 percent of the Area Median Income. The project is strategically located near major employment centers like Tesla's Gigafactory and Samsung's semiconductor facilities, and features a unit mix heavily weighted towards family-oriented floorplans with three- and four-bedroom layouts. This marks The NRP Group's first tax credit partnership and third overall collaboration with the Housing Authority of Travis County, underscoring a commitment to expanding affordable housing in high-growth areas.

data_points.notable_features: All units are reserved for households earning up to 60 percent of the Area Median Income (AMI); more than 60 percent of residences offer three- and four-bedroom layouts; 18 acres dedicated to residential use, with the remaining floodplain land dedicated to the City of Austin Parks Department for trail connectivity.
data_points.project_notes: This is The NRP Group’s first tax credit partnership with the Housing Authority of Travis County and its third collaboration overall with the agency. Safehold is participating as a ground lessor, marking the first use of its ground lease structure for an affordable multifamily development in Texas.

articles.title: Principal of Minneapolis’ Davis earns C-Level and Leadership Award
articles.narrative: Mark Davis, founder and principal of Minneapolis-based Davis, was honored with the inaugural C-Level and Leadership Award by Finance & Commerce, a Twin Cities business news publication. The award recognizes executives for exceptional leadership, integrity, vision, operational excellence, and community engagement. Davis's firm specializes in healthcare real estate, offering development, investment, brokerage, property management, and consulting services nationwide, with a focus on consistent communication, transparency, and relationship-driven execution.

articles.title: Stream Realty Partners celebrates groundbreaking of Northfield Logistics Center in Houston
articles.narrative: Stream Realty Partners has broken ground on Northfield Logistics Center, a new Class A industrial development in Houston's infill Northwest submarket. The project will deliver over 300,000 square feet of distribution space in a supply-constrained corridor, addressing strong occupier demand for modern logistics facilities. The development is strategically located with excellent access to major transportation routes and a dense labor pool, making it a significant addition to the area's industrial offerings.

data_points.notable_features: Class A distribution space with direct access to U.S. 290 via W 43rd Street and secondary access at Langtry Street, located within a dense labor pool and surrounded by retail and dining amenities.

articles.title: Roller Rabbit skates past Nantucket chain store ban
articles.narrative: Roller Rabbit has signed a two-year lease for a new retail location at 44 Centre Street in Nantucket, MA, circumventing the town's "formula business" bylaw that restricts chain stores. The company rebranded the store as "Roller Rabbit General Store" and committed to selling at least half non-Roller Rabbit branded products to comply with local regulations. This move follows a previous closure of a Roller Rabbit store in Nantucket due to the same bylaw, and its return has sparked debate among local residents and businesses regarding potential loopholes for other chains.

data_points.notable_features: Located in Nantucket's downtown district, subject to a local "formula business" bylaw restricting chain stores.

people.name: Hānnah Kinser-Sampedro
people.title: Vice President of Visual Merchandising and Store Design

articles.title: House knocks build-to-rent provision from amended Senate bill
articles.narrative: The House of Representatives has released an amended version of the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, notably removing a controversial provision that would have required institutional investors to sell build-to-rent homes within seven years of construction. This change is seen as a potential victory for the build-to-rent sector, which had experienced a freeze in investment due to the original provision. While restrictions on institutional investors in the single-family housing market remain, the definition of what constitutes such a home has been narrowed. The bill now faces a floor vote in the House, with its ultimate fate dependent on Senate approval.

articles.title: What the state budget means for real estate
articles.narrative: New York State lawmakers are nearing a final agreement on a $268 billion budget, which includes several significant real estate-focused proposals. Key among these are a new pied-à-terre tax targeting luxury second homes and a tax on all-cash home purchases over $1 million in New York City. Additionally, the budget is expected to include reforms to the State Environmental Quality Review Act (SEQRA) aimed at streamlining housing and infrastructure development by exempting certain projects from environmental review. These measures are intended to generate revenue for the city's budget deficit and accelerate housing construction, though some proposals have drawn criticism from the real estate industry.

companies_people.label: OWNER
companies_people.firm_name: New York City Department of Finance

companies_people.label: OTHER
companies_people.firm_name: Real Estate Board of New York

articles.title: Josh Flagg’s family firm looking to flip Soho retail property for $12M
articles.narrative: Flagg Family Capital, the investment firm owned by the family of residential broker Josh Flagg, is marketing a renovated retail property in Soho for just under $12 million. The firm, in partnership with Andrew Aryeh and David Elbaz, acquired three floors of retail space at 170 Mercer Street less than two years ago for an undisclosed price, though public records indicate prior transactions totaling $4 million. The property has been redeveloped into a two-story retail space with high ceilings and is fully leased to a fashion house on a 10-year term with annual escalations.

data_points.address: 170 Mercer Street, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Renovated into a two-story retail space with 24-foot ceilings and exposed red brick, located in a luxury residential loft building with 25 feet of frontage on Mercer Street. The space is fully leased to a designer fashion house for 10 years with 3% annual escalations.
data_points.project_notes: The property was previously acquired by entities tied to Andrew Aryeh and David Elbaz in two separate transactions totaling $4 million. Flagg Family Capital declined to confirm their purchase price but stated it was an exciting acquisition with partners Aryeh and Elbaz. The renovation involved removing the third floor.

articles.title: Rabsky buys troubled Tribeca development site for $30M
articles.narrative: The Rabsky Group has acquired a long-troubled development site at 267 Broadway in Tribeca for $30 million, with plans to construct a luxury mixed-use condominium project. The site, which has approved plans for a 45-story condominium and hotel tower, faced financial difficulties when the previous owner defaulted on its mortgage and the lender subsequently collapsed. This acquisition marks another significant Manhattan investment for Rabsky, a firm known for its prolific development activity in Brooklyn.

data_points.notable_features: Site has approved plans for a 45-story condominium and hotel tower overlooking City Hall Park.
data_points.project_notes: The previous owner defaulted on a $25 million mortgage in 2023, leading to foreclosure proceedings and receivership in 2025 after the original lender, Signature Bank, collapsed.

articles.title: WATCH: Real estate titans talk shop at TRD’s NYC Real Estate Forum
articles.narrative: This article summarizes key discussions from The Real Deal's NYC Real Estate Forum, highlighting various industry leaders' perspectives on critical issues. Related Companies CEO Jeff Blau defended New York City's market strength, citing Hudson Yards' success while advocating for tax and regulatory reform. Real estate brokerage leaders Pam Liebman and Bess Freedman debated transparency in listings, with Freedman pushing for industry-wide openness and Liebman supporting seller discretion. Vornado's Glen Weiss reaffirmed the firm's commitment to a major development project, echoing sentiments about political challenges. City officials, including HPD Commissioner Dina Levy and Deputy Mayor for Housing Leila Bozorg, addressed landlord relations and rent regulation, respectively. Bally's chairman Soo Kim discussed the company's successful casino bid in the Bronx, attributing it to strategic planning and local connections.

articles.title: Tavros Capital starts reimagining of beleaguered 250 Water
articles.narrative: Tavros Capital is moving forward with its redevelopment of 250 Water Street in the Seaport District, securing a significant lease with Chelsea Piers Fitness for a 76,000-square-foot health and wellness facility. This lease marks a key step in the project's progress since Tavros and Atlas Capital acquired the site last year. The development is envisioned as a comprehensive neighborhood asset, incorporating residential, commercial, and outdoor spaces.

data_points.address: 250 Water Street, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Five-story health and wellness facility with indoor and outdoor spaces.
data_points.project_notes: The site was previously acquired by Tavros and Atlas Capital for $150 million. The overall development will include 600 apartments, with 25% designated as affordable, along with commercial and outdoor space. Atlantic Retail is marketing 35,000 square feet of ground-floor retail space.

articles.title: Miamians sue to stop Trump presidential library in downtown Miami, again
articles.narrative: A group of Miami residents has filed a lawsuit to halt the development of a proposed Trump presidential library in downtown Miami, alleging the land transfer for the project is unconstitutional. This marks the second legal challenge against the project, with plaintiffs arguing that the inclusion of a potential hotel and the use of the Trump Organization's branding indicate an intent to monetize the skyscraper for personal profit, violating the Emoluments Clause. The lawsuit also cites concerns about the tower's impact on neighborhood views and living conditions.

data_points.notable_features: Adjacent to the Freedom Tower and steps from Kaseya Center arena and the bay.
data_points.project_notes: The site was first transferred by Miami Dade College to the state, then gifted by Governor Ron DeSantis and the Florida Cabinet to the Trump library foundation. The site is valued between $67 million and $300 million.

articles.title: Rishi Kapoor admits to stealing $85M from investors, vows to make it his “life’s work” to repay them
articles.narrative: Rishi Kapoor, founder of Location Ventures, has admitted to misappropriating $85 million from investors, including siphoning funds for personal use, as part of a plea deal with federal prosecutors. He pleaded guilty to two charges of money laundering and payroll tax evasion conspiracy. The scandal, which began three years ago, involved Kapoor unlawfully using deposits from pre-construction condo unit buyers and paying himself millions without shareholder approval. A court-appointed receiver is currently selling Location Ventures' assets to repay creditors.

data_points.notable_features: Funds were unlawfully used from buyers of 20 pre-construction units in projects across Coconut Grove, Coral Gables, and Miami Beach.
data_points.project_notes: Rishi Kapoor admitted to misappropriating $85 million from investors and collecting millions in unauthorized salary, using some funds for a yacht, ring, and luxury home. A court-appointed receiver is selling Location Ventures' assets to repay creditors as part of a related federal civil lawsuit by the SEC.

articles.title: Miami Beach board greenlights plans for Terra, partners’ bayfront condo tower
articles.narrative: Terra and its partners, RG Development Group and GV Development, received unanimous approval from the Miami Beach Design Review Board for a 330-foot-tall luxury condo tower in South Beach. The project, located at 1250 West Avenue, will feature 106 condo units averaging nearly 3,500 square feet, replacing a 1964-built condo building that Terra and former partner Michael Stern acquired last September for $120 million. The development also includes a ground-floor commercial space and tropical gardens, with the developer committed to mitigating construction impacts and completing a baywalk behind the site.

data_points.notable_features: Luxury condo tower with units averaging nearly 3,500 square feet, including two penthouses with rooftop pools and private amenity decks. Features underground parking, a 2,685-square-foot commercial space, and tropical gardens with pollutant-filtered bio swales.
data_points.project_notes: The site was previously acquired for $120 million in September, financed with a $98 million loan from Northwind, and included the Bikini Hostel property across the street. The project's original developer, Michael Stern, secured an overlay zoning district allowing for the 330-foot height in exchange for completing a baywalk and redeveloping the Bikini Hostel into a city park. Stern exited the project earlier this year.

articles.title: South Florida golf courses targeted in lawmakers’ latest land-use power grab
articles.narrative: Florida lawmakers have passed the Infill Redevelopment Act, a new piece of legislation that would remove local governments' power to block housing developments on recreational sites such as golf courses, tennis courts, and clubhouses. The act, which targets Miami-Dade, Broward, and Palm Beach counties, allows developers to proceed with staff-level administrative approval, bypassing public hearings, provided the sites have been closed for at least a year and developers pay double the standard impact fees. While intended to streamline development and potentially reduce costs, the legislation notably lacks an affordability mandate, a key difference from the state's earlier Live Local Act which requires 40% workforce housing.

articles.title: Baird & Warner continues dynasty with Lucy Baird named chief stewardship officer

people.name: Lucy Baird
people.title: chief stewardship officer and vice chair of the board

articles.title: Amazon’s massive retail store development in Oak Brook passes first test
articles.narrative: Amazon is moving forward with plans for a large-format retail store in Oak Brook, Illinois, after the village panel recommended approval for the project. The development, which involves rezoning and the demolition of existing office buildings, represents a strategic shift for Amazon into physical retail, moving beyond its previous smaller-format store concepts. The company aims to create a unique shopping experience, with completion anticipated by October 2028.

data_points.notable_features: Costco-style grocery and merchandise store, one of the first of its kind in the country.
data_points.project_notes: Requires zoning changes from office to retail. Involves the demolition of seven office buildings totaling approximately 600,000 square feet.

articles.title: Tishman Speyer lists 357-unit Union West apartments in Fulton Market
articles.narrative: Tishman Speyer has listed its 357-unit Union West apartment building in Chicago's Fulton Market for sale, three years after acquiring it for $128 million. The 15-story property, located at 939 West Washington Boulevard, is currently 96% leased, with its 12,436-square-foot retail component fully occupied. The decision to sell comes amidst a robust Chicago multifamily market, characterized by strong demand and significant investment activity, including numerous office-to-residential conversions.

data_points.notable_features: 15-story building with 12,436 square feet of fully leased retail space.
data_points.project_notes: Previously acquired by Tishman Speyer in 2023 for $128 million.

articles.market_intelligence: Chicago's multifamily market has seen $6 billion in acquisitions over the past year. Demand for apartment space is strong, leading to an increasing number of office-to-residential conversions in Downtown Chicago. Fulton Market, a former meatpacking district, continues to experience accelerated growth in high-end retail, apartment, and office developments.

articles.title: Gresham James Hotels purchases Home2 Suites location on the River Walk
articles.narrative: Gresham James Hotels has acquired the 128-key Home2 Suites by Hilton San Antonio Riverwalk, marking a strategic expansion for the Dallas-based firm outside its primary North Texas market. The acquisition was financed with a $16.5 million loan, and the new owner plans to undertake renovations to reposition the hotel for increased corporate and leisure travel. This transaction occurs amidst a challenging period for San Antonio's tourism industry, which has seen declines in hotel revenue and occupancy.

data_points.property_name: Home2 Suites by Hilton San Antonio Riverwalk
data_points.notable_features: Located on the San Antonio River Walk.
data_points.project_notes: The hotel was last valued at $10 million by the Bexar Central Appraisal District.

articles.market_intelligence: San Antonio's tourism industry experienced a 7 percent drop in hotel revenue in 2025, with 2.5 million room nights sold in Q4 2025, a decrease of approximately 220,000 room nights year-over-year from 2024. Hotel occupancy also declined by more than 5 percent.

articles.title: Houston’s upcoming Ritz-Carlton Residences selling condos at record pace
articles.narrative: The Ritz-Carlton Residences in Houston's Uptown district has achieved over $203 million in pre-construction condo sales within four months of its announcement, including a $30 million penthouse that may be the highest-priced condo sale in Texas history. The 45-story tower will feature 112 branded residences and a 156-key hotel, with completion anticipated in fall 2029. The strong sales, primarily from local buyers, highlight the increasing demand for luxury branded condominiums in the Houston market.

data_points.notable_features: 600-foot-tall, 45-story tower with 27,000 SF of resident amenities and 50,000 SF of hotel amenities, including pet care, fitness center, heated lap pools, and a restaurant bar.
data_points.project_notes: Penthouse sold for $30 million, potentially the highest-priced condo ever sold in Texas. Over $203 million in sales achieved before groundbreaking, with $53 million in sales in the last 30 days. Approximately 80% of purchases are from Houston and surrounding suburbs.

articles.market_intelligence: Satya's St. Regis condo building at 102 Asbury Street previously held the record for most expensive condo sold in Houston at $14.5 million. The Randall Davis Company is developing a 15-story, 33-unit condo building nicknamed 'Chaucer' at 2360 Rice Boulevard for $65 million.

articles.title: As Texas data center gold rush reaches fever pitch, so does local resentment
articles.narrative: The rapid expansion of data center development in Texas, particularly in Caldwell County, is generating significant local opposition despite an estimated $20 billion in investment over the next five years. Local officials and residents are concerned about the strain on water and the state's electrical grid, with data centers accounting for 87% of new large load connection requests to ERCOT. While some local governments are pushing back, the state legislature, which will not reconvene until next year, shows little inclination to regulate the AI-driven development boom, leaving communities with limited recourse.

articles.title: Land purchases inch embattled Thakkar family’s Mustang Square closer to completion
articles.narrative: Thakkar Developers has sold additional commercial and residential parcels within its Mustang Square mixed-use project in Plano, TX, bringing the development closer to full build-out. Foundry Plano, an office development company, acquired a 3.5-acre lot to construct 13 luxury office condominiums. Separately, homebuilder D.R. Horton purchased four more homesites, adding to its existing holdings in the residential section of the development. The sales occur as Thakkar Developers faces ongoing legal challenges, including a tax lawsuit in New York and federal fraud charges against its principals.

data_points.project_notes: The residential component of the development was previously planned for townhomes but changed to detached single-family homes. A fitness center is also under construction within the development.

articles.title: Ashford Hospitality Trust sells another hotel in its expansive portfolio for $17M
articles.narrative: Ashford Hospitality Trust has sold the 150-key Embassy Suites by Hilton Dallas to DG Lodging for $17 million, or approximately $133,000 per key. This transaction is part of Ashford's strategy to divest six hotels from its portfolio, with this sale marking the fifth completed disposition since April. The REIT aims to streamline its holdings, with one additional sale pending.

data_points.project_notes: Ashford Hospitality Trust received approximately $16.6 million after selling expenses and paid an additional $16 million to the mortgage lender.

articles.title: More Oakland offices seized by lender as East Bay distress grows
articles.narrative: Edgewater Park Plaza, a 206,000-square-foot office complex in Oakland, was seized by its lender, East West Bank, through foreclosure after the owner defaulted on $21.4 million in debt. The property, valued at $8.7 million in the foreclosure, represents a significant decline from its 2022 purchase price of $35.7 million, highlighting the severe distress in Oakland's office market due to high interest rates, weak leasing demand, and remote work trends.

data_points.notable_features: Office campus located near the airport.
data_points.project_notes: Property was previously acquired for $35.7 million in 2022. Unpaid debt totaled $21.4 million at the time of foreclosure.

articles.market_intelligence: Oakland's office market is experiencing significant distress, with values cratering due to higher interest rates, weak leasing demand, and remote work. Vacancies are mounting and tenant demand is languishing, making it one of the Bay Area's hardest-hit office markets.

articles.title: Union Square comeback continues with 14K sf Market Street museum lease
articles.narrative: The Museum of Illusions has signed a 14,300-square-foot lease at 735 Market Street in San Francisco, occupying a space that has been vacant since 2020. This lease is a significant step in the ongoing revitalization of the Market Street corridor and the broader Union Square neighborhood, which has seen increased foot traffic and declining vacancies. The museum, known for its interactive and social-media-friendly exhibits, is part of an aggressive U.S. expansion strategy by its new investor group.

data_points.notable_features: The space was formerly occupied by Decathlon and has been vacant since 2020.
data_points.project_notes: Tenant improvements are estimated to cost $1.6 million.

articles.market_intelligence: Foot traffic in the Union Square submarket increased 9.6% year over year, and vacancies fell 2.8% year over year.

articles.title: Brookfield snaps up South Bay data center for $90M, adding to growing South Bay collection
articles.narrative: Brookfield Properties has acquired a 120,000-square-foot data center in Sunnyvale, CA, for $90.3 million from DivcoWest. The property, located at 255 Caspian Drive, is fully leased to data center operator Equinix. This acquisition further expands Brookfield's growing portfolio of data centers in the South Bay, reflecting the increasing demand for AI-serving infrastructure in the region.

data_points.project_notes: DivcoWest previously acquired the property in 2017 for $50 million.

articles.market_intelligence: The South Bay is a hub for data center development, with San Jose identified as a hotspot. Prologis plans 1.7 million square feet of data centers across four buildings in San Jose, and Goodman Group plans two data centers totaling 414,000 square feet. Menlo Equities is pivoting a previously approved 400,000-square-foot office and life sciences campus at 888–894 Ross Drive in Sunnyvale to a 200,000-square-foot data center with an adjacent PG&E substation.

articles.title: Realterm Acquires Hunts Point Outdoor Storage Facility
articles.narrative: Realterm, a global investment manager specializing in transportation-focused properties, has acquired a one-acre industrial outdoor storage (IOS) maintenance facility in The Bronx's Hunts Point submarket. The property, which includes a 9,400-square-foot maintenance building, is currently vacant and will undergo capital improvements to enhance its functionality and appeal. This acquisition aligns with Realterm's strategy of investing in transportation-advantaged IOS facilities within supply-constrained markets like Hunts Point, which serves as a critical supply chain hub for New York City.

data_points.address: 935 Garrison Ave., New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Fully paved, lit, and secure industrial outdoor storage facility with three drive-in bays.
data_points.project_notes: Property is currently vacant and available for lease. Realterm plans targeted capital improvements.

articles.title: Hollywood Multifamily Sale Reaches Three-Year High in Pricing
articles.narrative: Colliers brokered the sale of Sophia Hollywood, a 28-unit Class A multifamily community, for approximately $16 million. This transaction represents the highest price per unit achieved in Hollywood in over three years, signaling a growing trend among investors who view current market conditions as a buying opportunity. The property's newer construction, condominium-quality design, and larger floor plans were key differentiators, making it attractive to investors despite elevated development costs and interest rates.

data_points.notable_features: Class A property with newer construction, condominium-quality design, and larger floor plans.

articles.title: Chicago-Based Stotan Industrial Launches NASA Causeway Logistics Center in Florida
articles.narrative: Stotan Industrial, in partnership with CrossHarbor Capital Partners, has acquired 28.63 acres in Titusville, Florida, for the development of the NASA Causeway Logistics Center. This project will feature two industrial buildings totaling 306,980 square feet, strategically located near the Kennedy Space Center and other key infrastructure. The development aims to capitalize on the strong regional demand driven by major space, port, and aviation operators.

data_points.notable_features: Located within Vector Space Business Park, adjacent to the NASA Causeway and approximately five miles from the Kennedy Space Center, providing access to Space Coast's launch, port, aviation, and highway infrastructure.
data_points.project_notes: The project will include two divisible industrial buildings: Building A (215,460 SF) and Building B (91,520 SF).

articles.title: Colliers Completes Sale of 321K-SF Kansas City Office Building
articles.narrative: Colliers has facilitated the sale of 2323 Grand Boulevard, a 321,000-square-foot office building in Kansas City's Crown Center submarket. Stanton Road Capital, LLC sold the 11-story property to Crain Company, a Wichita-based multifamily acquisition and development firm. The transaction highlights the area's strong long-term potential, driven by its prime location, panoramic downtown views, and proximity to significant new developments like the Kansas City Royals stadium.

data_points.notable_features: 11-story building with panoramic downtown views, covered and surface parking, and walkable access to hotels, retail, and dining.
data_points.project_notes: Property was acquired by Stanton Road Capital in 2017.

articles.title: CIM Group Launches Platform To Invest In Rural QOZ Power Projects
articles.narrative: CIM Group has launched Permanent Power Co., a national platform focused on investing in rural qualified opportunity zones to develop power projects. This initiative aims to consolidate CIM Group's efforts in solar generation, energy storage, and transmission infrastructure, addressing the increasing power demands from sectors like artificial intelligence and data centers. The new company has secured $400 million in financing from HPS Investment Partners to advance its growth and accelerate the development of its initial solar projects in California's San Joaquin Valley.

data_points.notable_features: Projects located within a 20,000-acre solar park in California's San Joaquin Valley; focused on rural qualified opportunity zones.

articles.title: BGO Gives Up On Nontraded Industrial REIT That Never Drew Buyers
articles.narrative: BGO has terminated its nontraded REIT, BGO Industrial Real Estate Income Trust (IREIT), nearly three years after its launch, having failed to attract any outside investors. The IREIT, seeded with a 56.5% interest in a 9.4M SF industrial portfolio, had $197.3M in total assets at the end of 2025 but incurred over $3M in losses in each of the last two years. The decision to terminate comes as the portfolio's assets face upcoming loan maturity dates and amid a challenging environment for nontraded REITs, which saw redemption caps by major players like Blackstone and Starwood.

data_points.property_name: BGO Industrial Real Estate Income Trust
data_points.notable_features: Portfolio of 29 industrial properties spread across the Midwest, including multibuilding developments in St. Louis, Cincinnati, and Kansas City, and a single 757K SF property in the Chicago area.
data_points.project_notes: Launched in 2023 with a $560M senior loan in two tranches at four- and five-year terms. Sun Life Financial contributed a 56.5% interest in the portfolio to seed the fund.

companies_people.label: OWNER
companies_people.firm_name: BGO Industrial Real Estate Income Trust

articles.market_intelligence: Nontraded REIT fundraising experienced a period of decline, with major players like Blackstone Real Estate Income Trust (BREIT) and Starwood Real Estate Income Trust (SREIT) capping redemptions due to investor concerns over commercial real estate valuations. BREIT has since cleared its backlog and saw its highest quarterly capital raise in three years in Q1, while SREIT continues to face significant outstanding redemption requests.

articles.title: Related Midwest Associate Returns To Competition That Helped Him Break Into CRE
articles.narrative: This article highlights the Eisenberg Real Estate Challenge as a crucial platform for young talent to enter the commercial real estate industry, especially in a tightening job market. DuWarren Gibson, an associate at Related Midwest and a former participant, returned as a judge, emphasizing the competition's role in providing real-world development experience and fostering valuable industry connections. The challenge, now in its 16th year, tasks student teams with creating project proposals for actual development sites, such as an 8.1-acre parcel within Related's The 78 megadevelopment in Chicago. The article underscores the increasing importance of relationship-based hiring and networking for recent graduates, particularly as AI impacts the job market and entry-level CRE positions decline.

articles.title: 65-Year-Old FREIT To Liquidate
articles.narrative: The First Real Estate Investment Trust of New Jersey (FREIT), a REIT incorporated in 1961, has announced a plan of voluntary liquidation, which will involve selling off its entire portfolio of assets. The portfolio includes six apartment complexes, five shopping centers, and three parcels of vacant land in New Jersey, as well as a residential property in Middletown, New York. After liabilities are paid, net proceeds will be distributed to stockholders, with payments estimated between $24.44 and $30.03 per share, representing a significant premium over its recent stock price. The company's CEO noted pride in its legacy of consistent returns and expressed optimism about returning capital to stockholders in the current real estate environment.

data_points.notable_features: Portfolio includes six apartment complexes, five shopping centers, and three parcels of vacant land in New Jersey, plus one residential property in New York.
data_points.project_notes: FREIT's board of directors unanimously approved a plan of voluntary liquidation. Stockholders will vote on the plan in the fall of 2026.

companies_people.label: OWNER
companies_people.firm_name: First Real Estate Investment Trust of New Jersey (FREIT)

articles.market_intelligence: Other publicly traded landlords, including Elme Communities and Stratus Properties, have also announced liquidation plans, citing undervaluation in the current market. Many public real estate companies are being taken private or merging with competitors, while fewer companies are pursuing IPOs due to sustained private capital growth.

articles.title: Vanderbilt Office, Hillwood And TriPost Capital JV Buys 1.4M SF Office Campus In DFW
articles.narrative: A joint venture formed by Vanderbilt Office Properties, Hillwood Urban, and TriPost Capital Partners has acquired The Towers at Williams Square, a 1.4M SF Class-A office campus in Irving, TX. The iconic property, known for its distinctive architecture and the Mustangs of Las Colinas sculpture, has undergone over $30M in renovations and is home to a strong tenant mix. The buyers believe the campus is well-positioned to capitalize on the ongoing flight-to-quality trend in the office market, driven by its amenities, accessibility, and central location within the Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex.

data_points.notable_features: Class-A office campus featuring a 26-story center high-rise, two 14-story towers, an adjacent five-story building, and the Mustangs of Las Colinas bronze sculpture.
data_points.project_notes: Over $30M in renovations have been completed over the years. Vanderbilt Office Properties previously partnered with Apollo Global Real Estate to purchase the campus in 2016 for $330M, with Hillwood also involved in that prior deal.

people.name: Ariel Guerrero
people.title: Regional Manager of Market Intelligence for the Central Region

articles.market_intelligence: DFW's flight-to-quality trend pushed asking rents for trophy office buildings to record highs last year. Class-A office asking rents rose by nearly 4% year-over-year in the first quarter across all competitive tiers.
